title: LanzaTech Selects Saltend for Â£600M SAF Project "DRAGON II"
content: LanzaTech has selected px Groupâ€™s Saltend Chemicals Park in Humberside as the location for its "DRAGON II" project, a Â£600 million investment dedicated to producing Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). The facility is designed to produce approximately 80,000 tonnes of SAF annuallyâ€”meeting roughly 1% of the UK's jet fuel demandâ€”along with 8,000 tonnes of renewable diesel. Utilizing LanzaJetâ€™s Alcohol-to-Jet (AtJ) technology, the project will convert ethanol, derived from waste carbon dioxide and green hydrogen via LanzaTech's gas fermentation process, into Power-to-Liquid (PtL) fuel. Supported by a Â£6.4 million grant from the UK Department for Transport, construction is scheduled to begin in the second half of 2027, with operations starting in 2030. The initiative is expected to create 300 construction jobs and 150 permanent roles, reinforcing Humbersideâ€™s position as a leader in industrial decarbonization.
author: Analysts Account
